title: rootlessport is still used instead of pasta when running  a container connected to a bridge network.
body:
### Issue Description

if we running a container connected to a bridge network,  Ports are exposed using `rootlessport` instead of `pasta` 
for eg: if we are running following command 
`podman run -d --rm --network=podman  --name srv1 -p 4080:80 docker.io/library/nginx` 
port `4080` is exposed using rootlessport mechanism. 

instead of that, if I run the following command, ( ie, remove network=podman part )
`podman run -d --rm  --name srv1 -p 4080:80 docker.io/library/nginx`  
ports are exposed using pasta and  public IP address of incoming traffic is available inside container. 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. in one terminal , run  `podman run --rm --network=podman --name srv0 -p 4080:80 docker.io/library/nginx:alpine`
2. in another terminal, run ` podman run --rm   --name srv1 -p 4081:80 docker.io/library/nginx:alpine`
3. Send some requests to both servers from an external IP.  `ab  -n10 http://poman-machine.lan:4080/` , `ab  -n10 http://poman-machine.lan:4081/` 



### Describe the results you received

Observe the output of 1st and second terminals. In first one, we can only see internal private IP in the access log. In the second terminal, we can see  IP of the external server in the access log. 

### Describe the results you expected

I was expecting public IP address of external server to appear in the access log of both containers.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

NA

### Additional information

The reported behavior makes pasta unusable in most of real-world situation, where every container will be connected to at-least a network. ( For eg: a container running using `compose` command )

In my understanding, if podman is using `pasta` to expose ports of container,  it should replace `rootlessport` . 
In my experience , `pasta` is able to expose ports of container even if the container is connected to bridge network. 

for eg, if we run the following command 
```
pasta  -f  --config-net -t 5080-5080:80-80 --dns-forward 169.254.1.1 -u none -T none -U none --no-map-gw    --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2 <PID_of_first_process_inside_container>
``` 
we can simply expose container's port to public using pasta.  
IMHO, we should do this instead of running `rootlessport`

comments.body:
That is the expected behavior, a proper fix is tacked in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8193

The design is shown here https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22943#discussioncomment-9795883

>we can simply expose container's port to public using pasta.
> IMHO, we should do this instead of running rootlessport

It is not that simple, that would break the routing and the container now has two interfaces instead of one.

@Luap99 : Thanks for your quick response. 

Regarding, 
> It is not that simple, that would break the routing and the container now has two interfaces instead of one.

I checked and understand that it will create multiple "default" route inside containers . 
But, I did run couple of `ip r get`  commands to check how routing behaves in the situation and found that everything works as expected 
What I did is summarized below . 
1. Created 3 containers inside single compose file ( 3 containers connected to single bridge network in rootless mode ) 
2. 80 port Container3  is exposed to public via pasta
3. Checked Container3 's web server is accessible from public or not . Found it is working 
4. Checked Container3 's web server is accessible from other containers  . Found it is working 
5. Checked Container3  can access public internet or not. Found it is working. ( The traffic is now going through pasta I think )
6. Checked Container3 can access other containers with in network . Found it is working. 

If multiple default routing is the problem, will it get fixed If we somehow remove the duplicate "default route" ( Already existing default route )  and fix the routing 
for eg: 
Private network traffic go through internal podman bridge network,
All other traffic go through interface created by `pasta` 

compose.yml file I used for experiment
```yaml
services:
  os0:
    image: 'simple-webserver'
    build:
      context: ./
    command:
      - 'nginx'
  os1:
    extends: os0
  os2:
    extends: os0
```
Dockerfile
```Dockerfile
FROM alpine:3.22.0

RUN apk update; \
  apk add nginx curl tcpdump netcat-openbsd iproute2 nginx

ENTRYPOINT [ "/bin/sh", "-c", "trap 'echo Shutting down; killall; exit 0' SIGTERM SIGINT SIGKILL; echo starting...; for i in \"$@\";  do sh -c \"$i\" & done;  sleep infinity & wait", "--" ]
```

Just the fact that there is a second interface could already break someone potentially.

Then the route itself might causee issue because custom networks have a lot more options that need to be respected --internal, --route, --opt=metric --opt=no_default_route and more.
Sure it works for you but this is not a maintainable approach long term as it bypasses when the custom networks do.
